dialog.stepLabel: 播定镜C-收到总督宫邀约
dialog.nodeDesc: 播定镜C-收到总督宫邀约

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Woo-wee. Sure is a beautiful day.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: So any news from the Ephor, {PlayerName}? Or are we still chillin' like we've been for the past few days?

items.speaker: Ephor's Palace Attendant
items.text: Good day, {Male=Sir;Female=Madam} {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Ephor's Palace Attendant
items.text: I am Damian, an attendant in service to the Ephor's Palace. By direct command, I have come to deliver you Her Excellency's personal letter.

items.speaker: Ephor's Palace Attendant
items.text: It concerns a matter of utmost importance.

options.text: (Take the letter.)

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Me and my big mouth...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hey, what does it say? Scooch up a bit.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: It says Augusta has taken care of everything on her end. She's ready to meet us at the Ephor's Palace as agreed.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: It's most likely something to do with the <te href=750001>hunt</te>.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We shouldn't keep the Ephor waiting... Time to go, Abby.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Aww. You mean now?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Well, I think I'll sit this one out and leave all the heavy stuff to you guys. Hehe.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Chat for as long as you like. Just gimme me a holler if you need me.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hic. I can't eat another bite... Hehe. That's another full stomach for Abby.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Huh? Hey, {PlayerName}. Look! Over there!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: That's... a storm...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Which means it's about time to meet up with Augusta.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Tempest Cliffs. I remember that's where she told us to go.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Uh... Well... You better hurry!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Same as always, call me when you need me and I'll be out in a flash!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: This... should be the right way...
If you're heading for Tempest Cliffs, head east. This path will take you straight to the courtyard of glory.
Or perhaps, would you care to travel with me?
After all, we share the same destination. Your reputation precedes you, honorable {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: I am Gladiator Avidius. At your service.

options.text: Are you also here for the hunt?

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: "Gladiating" and "hunting" are the very pillars upon which the glory of Septimont stands. I believe no Gladiator would refuse such an honor if invited.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: I, of course, am no exception.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Please, if you'll follow me. Honored guest from afar.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Good day to you, {Male=Sir;Female=Madam} {PlayerName}. It seems you too have taken an interest in the Temple's murals.

options.text: These murals...

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: They are the works of past Priestesses. They depict prophecies glimpsed in the <te href=750009>Sacred Flames</te>—ones already fulfilled and ones yet to come.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: In any case, all these murals point to two prophecies of importance.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: The first is a Ragunnesi prophecy condemning Septimont's lack of faith in the Sentinel—

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: "Hear this, O faithless ones of Septimont. The Divinity of the high heavens has grown enraged. Should your monarch once more stand in contempt of Their will, annihilation shall be your fate."

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: The second is a continuation of the Ragunnesi prophecy, delivered by Septimont's Priestesses—

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: "Only the Hero of Heroes of extraordinary power, risen from the dark tides, shall bring eternity to Septimont. Then shall the waters quiet, the tempest still, and glory return with the blood of the old king."

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: You seem drawn to that particular mural... It tells of a calamity yet to come, and as always, the Hero of Heroes who shall rise among us to turn the tide of fate.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Yet the identity of this Hero remains a mystery. Coincidentally, much like the absent part of this mural, the prophecy of our victory over the coming disaster remains untold...

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Whether the Priestesses can't foresee the fate that awaits, or whether the true prophecy is meant to remain hidden... Is indeed a question worth pondering.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Our would-be Hero of Heroes, lost in daydreams again? What is it this time? Staring at an empty wall, hoping one day it'll bear your name?

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: I happened to wander by, passing the time as one does. No need to trouble yourself with someone as inconsequential as I, Priestess.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Inconsequential? Come now. Everyone knows you're the Senate's golden child. Those old loons wouldn't spare me a glance, but they hang on your every word.

options.text: Umm... Iuno...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Have you been waiting long? Ugh, I can't believe he got to you first.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Isn't paying respects to the Heroes fairly commonplace? Surely, we needn't allow rivalry to come between us, Priestess.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Hmph. I'm not blind to your tricks, Avidius. Do you really think {PlayerName} would take sides? With you?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Let me guess... Eliminating Tyrannos and the Dark Tide before anyone else would be the perfect act to prove you're the prophesied Hero of Heroes, hmm?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Four expeditions to <te href=750010>Manes Deep</te> plus a <te href=750011>Holmleaf Medal</te>, and now you think you can usurp Augusta's place? Don't make me laugh.

options.text: Manes Deep?
Holmleaf Medal?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Oh, read the room, will you?

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Please, I don't deny that I am ambitious and aspire to better my station. But I value my duty to defend Septimont's glory just as equally.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Should I become the next Hero, Septimont would be spared when the next calamity arrives. Is this not what the Tetragon Temple has prophesized?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Why you...

options.text: What if Augusta is the true Hero of Heroes?

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: She possesses many qualities worthy of a Hero. But she lacks the "extraordinary power" mentioned in the prophecy.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: As a Resonator, Augusta offers nothing special. Yet throughout Septimont's history, every Hero of Heroes was born with extraordinary gifts.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: She may have the strength to wield a sword, but I doubt her ability to endure pain and the slow, wearisome passage of time like other Resonators.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: It's a pity that in a city built on prophecy, Augusta is a disruptor of the rules.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: I assure you, Priestess, I will not act from the shadows. If Augusta seeks a fair challenge, I shall honor her with the respect she deserves.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Priestess, {PlayerName}, I beg your leave.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Perhaps... we will bear witness to the birth of a new Hero in the hunt to come.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Well, there you have it. That's the gist of it. Ugh, finally some time together, and now it's all ruined...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I don't understand. Given Augusta's prestige, the Senate has no real advantage in seizing power. What's their angle?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: It all started when Augusta shook things up the moment she became Ephor. Especially in rooting out corruption within the Gladiator's Agon while ignoring the senators in the process.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Naturally, it ruffled some feathers. Some even accused her of "undermining the spirit of Gladiating." In reality, they're just upset their piggy banks got smashed.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: More importantly...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: This is exactly the kind of chaos Augusta wanted. That idiot!

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: She even went so far as to say, "The citizens of Septimont only need to be loyal to Septimont, not her, Augusta."

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: And then, like it's nothing, she tells me that the Ephor position only matters when a strong Gladiator wants it.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: She's even more stubborn than I am.

options.text: Are you worried about Augusta's capabilities?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Now why would I be worried about that? I'm sure you've seen her sword. The inlaid blades are trophies of every Gladiator she's defeated.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Even ones with decent Resonance powers like Avidius, she eats for breakfast.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Though... Avidius makes me rather uncomfortable. It's got nothing to do with his stance, it's... more of a hunch. Something about him feels... unnatural.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Or maybe I'm just overthinking it.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: It's the <te href=750012>Griffrex</te>! It appears our prey has finally come out for some fresh air.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: We'll meet again later. But next time, I'd like your undivided attention. No distractions, hmm?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Is that lady over there... looking at me?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Better go and greet her.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Perfect timing.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I'll stretch my legs a bit.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I wonder what Grandmother Lillibet and {PlayerName} are talking about...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Never mind. It can wait.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Let's get eyes on this beast, Aquila.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Spirits of the hallowed land. Answer my call.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: May the eternal Sacred Flames grant us boundless sight.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Everything is in place.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It will take the bait.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: You can set me down here.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Come on... Don't give me that concerned look of yours. It's nothing to worry about.

options.text: You could stand to rest more.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Wait. You have questions. Don't pretend you don't.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Well, spit it out. It's only a minor injury, I can manage.

options.text: I saw you fall into the Dark Tide.
I saw the moment of Augusta's death.
I saw the destruction of Septimont.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: So you've managed to "glimpse" that far? Or is there more?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Heh. Just how many more surprises do you have for me? But I suppose only you could manage something like that.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Grandmother Lillibet, the Head Priestess you met at the Temple, once spoke of the "unknowable void" that is your fate.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I've yet to fully interpret what she meant by that, but one thing is clear. You are the most unique among us all.

options.text: What were those visions?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: They were... as I once perceived through the Sacred Flames... The fate of Septimont...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Just as you saw, the Dark Tide will bring destruction to Septimont, and... the death of Augusta.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I don't know whether Ragunna's predictions are actually true, but fate has given me no response but sorrow.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Though I would deny it to the end, as it is now... I cannot ignore it any longer. The Dark Tide is a force to be reckoned with.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Its terror lies not in the illusions it shows, or the lies it spins. Rather, it uncovers the parts of us we hide the most carefully. The parts of ourselves we least wish to face.

options.text: And if the fate you've glimpsed turns out to be false?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Never. My prophecies are never wrong.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: How I... loathe this feeling. This helplessness. To know everything, to see everything, and yet be powerless to change a thing...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Since we've come this far, might as well share a secret with you.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: The Iuno you see before you can no longer see the threads of fate.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I gave up my ability to peer into fate in "exchange" for the Resonance power to resist the Dark Tide. Why? Because I refused to be fate's obedient puppet, bowing to its whims and letting it dictate my every step.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: But in the end... despite all the sacrifice, we still couldn't escape its grasp...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: The dark figure we faced in Asphodel Barrens was, in fact, an embodiment of the Dark Tide. We call it the "False Sovereign."

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Unlike other Dark Tide creations, it can devour and reshape the scattered powers of its maker.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I'm unclear how things will unfold, but I'm certain it's the source of evil that will bring about the fall of Septimont, just as the prophecy foretold.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Before this hunt, Augusta and I investigated such Dark Tide irregularities at the Sanguis Plateaus.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: We then formed a plan to take advantage of the Sovereign's nature, using the Corrosaurus as bait to lure out its imperfect form...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Maybe this way, we can rewrite Septimont's tragedy before it even begins.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: But as you can see, we failed...

options.text: Is there really no way to alter fate and the prophecy?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I do not know...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Fate doesn't lie. Perhaps death is the only choice we're given in the end.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: But knowing Augusta... I'm sure that fool would fight till the bitter end.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: There is one thing... I'm not certain about. For reasons I can't explain, the fate shown to me by the Dark Tide feels different from what I remember.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: What is it exactly? Ugh...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: A side effect of my Resonance power.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: The Dark Tide doesn't exactly show up in a stable form. My powers manifest its creations in exchange for a teensy piece of my "existence." That's the price to pay, I suppose.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Ugh. I admit it sounds a bit complicated. In short, just know that a little sleep will relieve these side effects.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: A fair point. Once I've recovered my strength, sorting out these questions will be as simple as a flick of a finger.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Oh, by the way, take this.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: A Lunarum bracelet containing a small portion of my Resonance power. It was passed down to me by my predecessor when I became a Priestess.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: It's quite dear to me, you know, so be sure to take extra good care of it. And don't expect there will be a spare if you lose it.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Rely on it in my absence. Who knows, it may even help fill in for me and help you and Augusta in my place.

options.text: Thank you, Iuno.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Now, why would you say that?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I'll only be taking a short nap! Don't you or Augusta even dare think about leaving me behind, is that clear?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Now is not the moment

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Let it flee. The path it takes leads deeper into the trap we laid

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Impressive!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I expected a challenge, but your performance far exceeds what I had imagined.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Just as I thought, watching from the Colosseum's balcony is no way to understand a warrior's true spirit.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Only by crossing blades can I feel the will to fight pulsing through your veins.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Come. Step closer.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Laureate of Ragunna. Champion of Septimont. Favored Gladiator of <ano=Lioness of Glory>Arsinosa</ano>—by the honor of these titles, it is only right you stand by my side.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I, Augusta, Ephor of Septimont, offer you my highest esteem.

options.text: You've got a unique way of greeting guests.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Hehe. Simple and efficient, no need for pleasantries. Here, we let our blades do the talking. The Septimont way.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I have no doubt that you and I will have plenty of opportunities to get acquainted. But that's not why I called you here today...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: On my behalf, and that of the Ephor's Palace, I extend my deepest apologies for failing to keep our appointment.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I hope this delay hasn't dampened your enthusiasm for the hunt.

options.text: It was urgent. No need to apologize.
Your hospitality very much made up for it.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Haha. Tolerant and strong. Just as I've heard. Virtues of a true hero.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Well, then. Seeing that your interest still holds. Shall we discuss it?

options.text: Please.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The hunt—in Septimont's long fight for survival, it holds profound significance...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It is both honor and duty. For a hundred years, countless Gladiators have taken up the hunt and met their end in glory.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It's no exaggeration to say that the blood of Gladiators still burns in every inch of the crimson Sanguis Plateaus.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Our ancestors built the defenses that line those grounds. To this day, they stand as the frontline of Septimont's resistance against the Dark Tide.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: You've surely noticed the lingering Blightcloud that hovers over the Sanguis Plateaus. Since the first Dark Tide outbreak in Rinascita, that place has become a breeding ground for its <te href=750002>horrors</te>.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: As you've no doubt realized, our prey are Tacet Discords born from the Dark Tide. Only those who desecrate Septimont fall beneath the Gladiators' blades.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The High Tide is upon us... and it will be a storm like no other.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: My hunting party requires unstoppable warriors. Sacrifices are inevitable, but I believe you will be a banner that stands unbroken, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I share your sentiment. Septimont takes pride in its many remarkable Gladiators. But you, you are exceptional.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: A grand hunt awaits us, and my warriors and I long to do battle alongside a fighter such as yourself.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Of course, the will of the extraordinary deserves to be heard.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I'd like to know your thoughts on the matter, {PlayerName}.

options.text: I agree to join your hunt, but I have my own goal to accomplish.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: A straightforward answer. I like it.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: As for this "goal" of yours...

options.text: (Share what you've learned about the Sentinel's Relic and the Journeying Paradise.)

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Ah, yes, the Journeying Paradise. I've heard that laughable name before. A pity the hunt was made to shatter that little fantasy.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: As for the Sentinel's Relic...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Go to the <te href=750003>Earthrend Wedge</te>. It's where the Sentinel Imperator first descended upon the Sanguis Plateaus and is the "epicenter" of every hunt to this day.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: However, due to its significance, not even Septimont's highest officials are permitted near it.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But if that is what you wish... say no more. I will make it happen.

options.text: You have great trust in me.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The trust I place in you stems from your recognition of my character. I admire your honesty, and I will honor it to the fullest.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Only those bound by trust can go the distance together.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I look forward to the hunt ahead. It honors me to journey by your side.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Patience, my friend, is the first step to becoming a hunter.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: When the storm rages in the skies southeast of Septimont, I will await you at <te href=750004>Tempest Cliffs</te>.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: The pleasure of escorting you to the Ephor has been fulfilled. For now, my duty ends here.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: From here, we march onward to the hunting grounds, where the Dark Tide's creations may await us. When the time comes, it will be a true pleasure to see your skills in action.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Happy hunting.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Come. Join us, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The horns signal our departure.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Precisely. These cliffs mark the opening of our campaign.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Hahaha! Take courage, my friend.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The path to glory will reveal itself under the feet of the brave.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: The Hunter's Den. The busiest Gladiator encampment across the Sanguis Plateaus. Nearly every hunter passes through here on their way to the hunting grounds.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: We will rest here before setting off for the next hunt.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I must admit, this place is rather magnificent.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I suppose my predecessors forsook the peace they ought to have enjoyed, just to share this hard-earned land with these hunters. Such magnificence is rightly owed to the <te href=750005>Temple</te>.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Heh, the Priestess's words are not without merit. I am well aware that I stand under blessings, and will forever remember the sacrifices of the Temple.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Now if you'll excuse me. I'll be taking my leave.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Tch...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Though we've already met, I suppose a proper introduction is in order.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I am Iuno. Priestess of the Tetragon Temple.

options.text: I've heard about you. The Priestesses get to call the shots.
I've heard about you. The Priestesses have the gift of prophecy.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Well, aren't you caught up on things? What you say is true, though I've long since grown tired of such affairs.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Come now, {PlayerName}. Surely you can find me something more interesting to occupy my time. For instance... the hunt?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Quite frankly, when it comes to dealing with the Dark Tide, none of these Gladiators can hold a candle to me.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Ha. Look at those looks. I've seen them all before.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: What can I say? When you're as dazzling as I am, jealousy tends to follow.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I'll be making my way back, Augusta. Lest my shine further sting the eyes of certain people.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: If you find yourself with free time later, consider visiting me at Tetragon Temple.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Not every outlander is granted access to its sacred halls. Make the most of it.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It's rare to see Iuno hold back like that. You must be someone... different to her.

options.text: You two seem very close.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Perhaps... We do share some common traits.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Hehe. Don't let her unruly side fool you. She is as dependable as they come.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: A Corrosaurus.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Avidius was correct. It is a Tacet Discord born from the Dark Tide.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: What he didn't have the chance to tell you is that it is the deadliest Corrosaurus of them all—we call it "Tyrannos." A vanguard of the Dark Tide that rides its surge to reach our lands.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The Threnodian's attempt to plague Septimont never came to fruition. But do not mistake that for victory. Our enemy will not bow so easily.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The Dark Tide, immortal and indestructible, carries out the will of its creator—the Threnodian Leviathan.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Only the Earthrend Wedge that bears Sentinel Imperator's power can sever the link between the Dark Tide and the Threnodian, burying the calamity deep beneath the Sanguis Plateaus.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The "hunt" is how we disrupt the Dark Tide's frequencies by capturing and eliminating its creatures.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: However, the Earthrend Wedge's energy range is limited. Our mission is to deliver a true death to our prey.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Meaning we need to hunt and kill the beast near the Earthrender... Is that how we stop the Dark Tide from spreading?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Yes. And it must be done within <te href=750006>Maroonwood</te>.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: This confrontation with Tyrannos was expected, but it's not a fight anyone is eager to face...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: That beast is well aware of our plan. It's no mere prey. In many ways, it's a cunning hunter itself.

options.text: What's our plan for now?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Now we wait. We wait for it to enter Maroonwood.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The real hunt begins when the beast's hunger drives it to the wilds.

items.speaker: Ephor's Palace Attendant
items.text: Your Excellency, the Senators request your presence in the main tent.

items.speaker: Ephor's Palace Attendant
items.text: It's to discuss plans for the hunting operation...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Looks like I'll be occupied for a while.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Relax. Take a walk around camp. The <te href=750007>Hornett</te>'s blare will sound our next departure.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It's a sound that can be heard across the <te href=750008>canyons</te>. You won't miss it.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Over there.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Behind that cliff is the Earthrend Wedge. A relic of Sentinel Imperator as ancient as these lands.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The destination of your journey. My promise to you. Our shared goal.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: An <te href=750013>Anchor Stone</te> made from Tacetite formed around the Earthrender.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It contains a fraction of the Earthrender's power. It offers no defense capabilities, but it can at least serve as a temporary measure to hold off the Dark Tide or its creations.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: We need to deploy three of these in pre-determined locations across Maroonwood to generate a force field. This will drive the Corrosaurus to the <te href=750015>Asphodel Barrens</te> in the lower levels.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The field blooming with Asphodels is where the hunting party can safely reach the lower levels. Judging from the height, it's close to the Earthrender's base.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: In that field of glory, we will grant Tyrannos the death it deserves.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Tyrannos's calling card as the vanguard of the Dark Tide. Our prey isn't exactly known for playing by the rules.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Maroonwood lies on the other side of these cliffs. That beast has reasoned we're likely to take to the skies on Griffrexes and has laid an ambush, waiting to drag us down into the abyss.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: ...A Corrosaurus that knows how to set a trap, waiting for its hunters to walk right into it... Heh. Quite an interesting opponent.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Gladiators, march with me to the hunting grounds.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Let the Sacred Flames ignite your resolve! The enemies of Septimont shall bear witness to our might!

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: It... awaits you...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Let us be on our way.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: A field of corpses. Horrifying, isn't it?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: This is Tyrannos's birthplace. A kingdom of brutality, and the beast is proud of it.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Every hunt before this... it was here that we met our final battles.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: The Gladiators went to great lengths to drive the Dark Tide's creatures into the Asphodel Barrens. Likewise, their prey were just as eager to bait their hunters here.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: This is destined to be an evenly matched game of life and death. Hunter or prey? Only the one still standing gets to decide.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Avidius, descendant of the Hero of Heroes, Atilius. Decorated for the four crossings of Manes Deep.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I see the desire in you. Your desire for power.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Yet if you wish to be my opponent, then this level of resolve is far from enough.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Step forward. Stand next to me and pursue your desires without hesitation.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Let your ambitions burn even hotter.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: As you wish, Your Excellency.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Finally, some fun for a change.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Come. You need only to stay at my side, {PlayerName}. Rest.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: This is a matter between Augusta and Avidius. We shall not intervene for the time being.

items.speaker: Ephor's Palace Attendant
items.text: Messages have been sent to the encampment and all sectors of the main island, and defenses are currently being deployed per your instructions, Your Excellency.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Good. Ensure timely reports on any new developments.

items.speaker: Ephor's Palace Attendant
items.text: Yes, Your Excellency.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I've made arrangements for the war effort. Strategically, we must always brace ourselves for the worst.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Communications with the Black Shores are also underway.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The Ephor's Palace has previously established a connection with a Consultant in the Rinascita region. We've reached an agreement granting the Black Shores permission to access Septimont's Resonance Nexus. They'll be able to monitor and collect data on the Dark Tide during the hunt.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: In return, should any complications arise, the Black Shores are to fulfill their side of the agreement and provide us with the necessary technical support.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: A Consultant active in Rinascita?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: That's right. Her name is Buling. An interesting young lady. Left quite an impression on me.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: So it is her...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Why don't you let me handle contacting the Black Shores, Augusta?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We... talk from time to time. It'll help make things smoother for us.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Of course. I'll leave it to you then.

options.text: (Contact Buling.)

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Alright, I think I got the whole picture now... Oh {PlayerName}, how do you keep ending up in messes like this? This is one tough cookie, all right.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: You mean...

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Let me tell you, the latest data we got from the Resonance Nexus isn't so pretty. Something's really off with the Dark Tide in that Sanguis place. I don't remember it being this crazy before.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Normally, the Dark Tide getting all freaky during High Tide is whatever, right? But now, I don't know what happened, cuz it's... it's turning into a person.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: So listen up, I'll give you Tethys's rundown. That humanoid thing, that "Sovereign," is gobbling up frequencies like a maniac!

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: It's eating anything with energy. Dark Tide stuff, Tacet Discords, Echoes, you name it. Might even be saving other things for dessert.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Basically, the Sovereign isn't gonna attack Septimont or Ragunna anytime soon. It's too busy getting fat first. Once it's stuffed full of energy, it'll try and smash the Earthrend Wedge in one go.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: With the Earthrender gone, POOF—all that Dark Tide under the hunting grounds will explode like a pimple. When that happens, it's game over for Rinascita. Even if you have three heads and six arms, it will be bye-bye.

options.text: (Mention Augusta and Iuno's plan.)

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: You recall what Iuno once shared with you—the plan she and Augusta devised to hunt down the Sovereign. Though that plan has since failed, you relay the details to Buling, backed by Augusta's permit.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: But smooth moves, team. Cutting through the clutter, I see what you're doing.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: This Corrosaurus is just the bait to hook the big fish, isn't it? Your real plan is to stop the Sovereign before it fully forms, nip it in the bud.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Am I right, Boss?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: You are right on the mark.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Heaven and earth are in harmony... That's my reading, so your plan shouldn't have fallen apart like that.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: But then again... smooth sailing isn't always a good thing either. Too much harmony invites trouble. Tethys did pick up a strange frequency midway through the hunt.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Everything was fine until that frequency came out of nowhere, and suddenly the Sovereign took form way ahead of schedule. Talk about flipping the table.

options.text: Can you trace the frequency back to its source?

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Umm. Well. Let me get another reading... The Chi's all messed up, it's kind of hard to see.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: It's like someone clouded the heavenly signs on purpose...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: One way or another, we must find the Sovereign and stop it before it absorbs any more frequencies... This might be our only option for now.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: That's right. Supernatural powers or not, the Sovereign can't escape good old Tethys. Lemme see... Yep, it's wandering in an area with a whole lot of other frequencies...

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Looks like it's east of the Sanguis Plateaus. Yeah, that place has lots of dark energy. Bet many tough people died there.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Murmurstown...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Understood. I will stop it in its tracks.

options.text: If the Sovereign wants to destroy the Earthrender, I can't let that happen.
Under the oath of the hunt, I will fight alongside you to the very end.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Your Excellency.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: With your permission, I wish to accompany you to Murmurstown.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: I know this request may seem bold, and I'm aware of the dangers ahead. But as a warrior of Septimont, I cannot simply stand by.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Gladiators, we are united as defenders of Septimont's honor.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: I do not claim the right to ask this of you, but in my heart, I believe everyone here has earned the glory we bear. Let our deeds today reflect that greatness.

items.speaker: Veteran Gladiator
items.text: Well said, young man. Gladiators of Septimont fear no challenge. When the storm approaches, can an eagle soaring in the skies remain content as a fledgling beneath another's wings?

items.speaker: Veteran Gladiator
items.text: Your Excellency, we will follow you anywhere.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Very well. You have sworn your allegiance to Septimont with your resolve. Your bravery is witnessed not only by me, but by the fallen heroes lying in Capitoline Hill. They take pride in your courage.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: If you wish to fight together, then so be it. I will afford your fervor its due respect.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Come. Take up your blades—For Septimont. For the name of heroes eternal.

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: A wise choice, Augusta...

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: It is time. Return to where you belong. Fabianum has long awaited your homecoming...

items.speaker: Stunned Gladiator
items.text: W-What was that?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Steady... Now!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: What was that about? And that black fog... It was all mushy... I couldn't even finish it...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hic. Oh boy, did I take a huge bite outta that one. My head's gone all swishy.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ugh... I need a quick timeout, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: How are you holding up?
Augusta...

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: Augusta...

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: ...Now you've seen it. {Male=His;Female=Her} tiny little Echo. Silencing the Dark Tide with such ease... Resonance power. Such might. Such perfection.

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: Confined to this frail human form, you will achieve nothing.

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: You nearly lost everything... As you once did.

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: Augusta... do not resist the "Gift of Divinity." Follow our guidance. Return to <te href=750016>Murmurstown</te>...

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: To your home...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Augusta...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Augusta...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: If, and I say if... Septimont's fate has already been decided.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Would you continue to fight...?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: A ruler assumes their place... by accomplishing the impossible for their subjects.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It isn't safe here. I'll lead a team and escort everyone to a temporary camp not far from here. But before that, I must ensure no one is left behind.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: You go on ahead, {PlayerName}. I am entrusting Iuno to your care.

options.text: She'll be fine with me.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Is that...?
